sections.heading: Growth & Competition
sections.title: High performance organization roadmap for regulatory excellence
sections.summary: This section details the Financial Services Commission's strategic commitment to building a high-performing organization through talent development, operational efficiency, and stakeholder engagement. It mandates specific actions such as optimizing processes, implementing a 'Red Carpet' process, digitizing licenses, and enhancing regulatory compliance. The focus is on creating a resilient and agile regulatory framework that serves stakeholders effectively while minimizing operational disruptions.
sections.source_quote: High Performance Organisation : A Roadmap to Excellence

sections.heading: Resilience & Operational
sections.title: Digital transformation roadmap for financial regulators
sections.summary: This section outlines the Financial Services Commission's strategic commitment to digital transformation, including adopting a hybrid cloud strategy, strengthening SupTech solutions, and enhancing data analytics capabilities. It emphasizes the need for the commission to align IT solutions with customer expectations and regulatory requirements while improving data management and cross-border collaboration with international financial regulators.

requirements.text: Strengthen the use of SupTech solutions
Enhance data management, information retrieval, and analysis

sections.heading: International Relation &
sections.title: International collaboration strategies for financial regulators
sections.summary: This section focuses on the Financial Services Commission's strategic commitment to promoting global engagement and expertise sharing through international regulatory dialogues and collaborations. It mandates actions such as hosting quarterly strategic roundtables with industry leaders, collaborating with the Bank of Mauritius, and establishing effective communication channels with stakeholders to enhance the commission's global visibility and regulatory standing.

requirements.text: Host quarterly strategic roundtables with industry leaders
Collaborate with the Bank of Mauritius and other agencies

sections.heading: ESG / Green Transition
sections.title: ESG integration and innovation in financial services
sections.summary: This section outlines the Financial Services Commission's strategic commitment to embedding ESG considerations within financial innovation, including supervision of sustainability disclosures, enforcement against misconduct related to governance and greenwashing, and promoting Mauritius as a leader in socially responsible investment. It mandates actions such as introducing data-driven supervision solutions and adopting the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion charter.

requirements.text: Supervise sustainability disclosures and governance practices
Enforce measures against misconduct related to governance and greenwashing
Bolster Mauritius's position as a leading sustainable finance hub
Position Mauritius as a leader in Socially Responsible Investment

sections.heading: High-Performance Organisation
sections.title: AML/CFT compliance through enhanced supervisory frameworks
sections.summary: This section details the FSC's commitment to maintaining an effective risk-based AML/CFT supervisory framework aligned with international standards. It requires continuous enhancement of the FSC's capabilities, engagement with stakeholders, and implementation of real-time analytical tools for monitoring. The framework applies to licensees, professional bodies, and international regulatory bodies to ensure market integrity and consumer confidence.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates enhanced AML/CFT risk-based supervision with real-time monitoring tools, which are essential for assessing data transfers involving financial institutions that handle sensitive financial information across borders.

requirements.text: Continue engagement with private sector and stakeholders

sections.source_quote: Maintaining the Effectiveness of the FSC's AML/CFT/CPF Risk-Based Supervisory Framework in line with International Standards

sections.heading: Financial Literacy and Consumer
sections.title: Financial literacy and consumer inclusion initiatives
sections.summary: This section focuses on strengthening financial inclusion and consumer protection through educational programs, awareness campaigns, and collaboration with stakeholders. It requires the implementation of tech-savvy tools, participation in global financial inclusion initiatives, and regulations ensuring fair treatment. The initiatives apply to consumers, investors, licensees, and government regulators to promote financial literacy and combat fraud.
sections.dtia_summary: This section emphasizes financial literacy and consumer protection measures that impact data transfer practices by requiring awareness campaigns and fraud prevention, which are relevant for assessing data handling in financial services.

requirements.text: Improve use of tech-savvy tools and resources
Participate in regional and global Alliance for Financial Inclusion
Create awareness on Mauritius IFC's role

sections.source_quote: Strengthening Consumer Protection / Financial Inclusion • Improve the use of tech-savvy tools and resources
